name: Christmas Tree Bread
recipeInstructions:
Form the dough as directed in the recipe. Punch down the dough and divide it into four equal parts. Use two parts to make Fruit Nut Loaves (see instructions below). Roll out one part into a rectangle measuring 15x12 inches. Cut into two triangles, each with a base of 10 inches, a height of 12 inches, and sides of 15 inches. Place one triangle on a greased cookie sheet. Reshape if necessary. Spread about 1 cup of Fruit Nut Filling over the dough, leaving a 1/2-inch border around the edges. Moisturize the edges with water. Place the second dough triangle over the fruit-filled one and press to seal the edges. Roll out the remaining part into a thin rectangle (about 1/2 inch thick). Using a 2-1/2 inch doughnut cutter, cut 17 circles, removing the centers. Overlap the circles on top of the tree triangle. Roll the scraps into another 1/2-inch-thick piece and cut a 2-inch square to form the trunk. Cut an additional star shape for the top. Cover; let rise in a warm place for about 1 hour or until nearly doubled.
Bake in a 350°F oven for 35 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from the oven and brush cherries with glaze while still warm. Decorate the tree with decorator frosting. Makes one tree and two loaves.
Fruit-Nut Filling: In a small saucepan, combine all ingredients. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly for 1 minute.
Apricot Glaze: In a small saucepan, mix corn syrup and preserves. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly for 1 minute.
Fruit Nut Loaves: Grease two 8-1/2 x 4-1/2 x 3-inch loaf pans. Roll out each reserved quarter of dough into rectangles measuring 15 by 9 inches. Spread about 1 cup Fruit Nut Filling over each rectangle, leaving a 1/2-inch border around the edges. Roll up from the short side, seal the ends, and place seam-side down in prepared pans. Cover; let rise for about 1 hour or until doubled. Bake at 350°F for 40-45 minutes or until hollow when tapped. Remove from oven and brush with Apricot Glaze while warm.
